title: Holding a debate
setting: In a work meeting, you disagree with a proposal. The skill: persuade with logic, concede gracefully, stay composed.

steps.speaker: Colega
steps.es: Deberíamos lanzar el producto ya. No hay tiempo que perder.
steps.en: We should launch the product now. No time to lose.

options.es: Entiendo la urgencia, pero hay riesgos que considerar.
options.en: I understand the urgency, but there are risks to consider.
options.feedback: Validates his point first, then pivots — the hallmark of persuasive disagreement.
Blunt contradiction shuts down dialogue. Acknowledge, then make your case.
If you actually disagree, caving isn't persuasion. Hold your ground respectfully.

steps.speaker: Colega
steps.es: ¿Qué riesgos? El mercado está listo ahora.
steps.en: What risks? The market is ready now.

options.es: Concedo ese punto, sin embargo, las pruebas son incompletas.
options.en: I concede that point, however, the testing is incomplete.
options.feedback: 'Concedo... sin embargo' — conceding then countering is sophisticated debate structure.

options.es: El mercado no lo es todo.
options.feedback: A fair jab, but vague. Back it with the specific risk to be convincing.

options.en: I already told you, there are risks.
options.feedback: Repeating without evidence weakens you. Name the actual risk.

steps.speaker: Colega
steps.en: Well... so what do you propose then?

options.es: Una prueba breve primero; así reducimos el riesgo sin perder tiempo.
options.en: A short test first; that way we cut risk without losing time.
options.feedback: Offering a solution that honors HIS concern (time) too — that's how you win a debate.
A position, but no bridge to his urgency. Give him something he can say yes to.

options.en: I don't know, but not like this.
options.feedback: Opposing without an alternative leaves you stuck. Always bring a proposal.

options.es: Me alegra que lo veamos parecido. Trabajemos en eso.
options.en: I'm glad we see it similarly. Let's work on that.
options.feedback: Gracious in winning — framing it as 'we' keeps the relationship strong. Masterful.
Gloating undoes the goodwill you just built. Win humbly.

options.en: Perfect, let's move forward together then.
options.feedback: Collaborative and forward-looking. A clean, professional close.
